highlights.text: I wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “My general lean is that ES can hold 6592 though (or quick trap below).
Last week was a masterclass in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
One of those building blocks is the elevator down sell and this refers to a period of non-stop, rapid, headline driven selling.
The catch though, is they only occur when my core setup - the Failed Breakdown - triggers.
We saw it last Monday as stated: ES plunged to 6353 last Monday evening going elevator down .
An alternative is to wait for it to flush and recover (perhaps to 6353) for a Failed Breakdown.” We got this trigger at 830pm Monday evening, and were off to 6647 last weeks 6650 high week.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow

post_title: SPX Manages 1 Week of Green - First Since February. Can It Continue? April 7 Plan
